Instructional Integrity Studio

Cognitive safety for instruction systems

OverviewEvaluateHistory
Evidence-first evaluations
View evaluation history

Artifact evaluation

Paste onboarding guides, tutor prompts, or procedural docs. Each run is persisted with criterion-level rationale and next actions for your audit trail.

Focused task surface

The default screen should help the user complete one meaningful action immediately.

Saved run link appears after you evaluate

Evidence-first feedback

Results should name the criterion, show the status, and explain the exact next move.

Intent Preserved

Keep the objective statement, but consider adding a concrete outcome at the top.

PASS

The artifact states the objective early and does not drift into unrelated goals.

Assumptions Visible

Add a short section that names what the learner is expected to already know or have done.

WARN

The artifact assumes prior knowledge of the deployment environment but does not name it.

Sequencing Stable

Review the steps and ensure each action depends only on information already established.

PASS

The steps are ordered clearly and no dependent actions are collapsed.

Language Accessible

Swap any dense phrases for concrete verbs and examples that match your audience.

PASS

The wording is concrete and avoids dense jargon.

Neurodivergent Safe

Split long paragraphs into smaller task units with clear headings or bullet points.

WARN

One section contains a paragraph that should be split into smaller task units.

UI build target: learn the framework, evaluate artifacts, and move directly into implementation packages.
